summary: Recreate Next.js App Router + TypeScript + Bun with a single bounded context for todos: pure domain and application use cases, SQLite persistence behind ports, App Router routes/server actions as adapters, and Atomic Design + styled-components at the UI edge. Migrations version the schema; validation and errors stay at server boundaries with user-safe messages. This increment adds infra-owned dev governance: gitignored `.data/` and `.env.local`, documented env + SQLite lifecycle in README, `.env.example` with TODO_SQLITE_PATH, and a safe `.env.local` bootstrapâ€”without duplicating SQLite adapters outside backend/infrastructure.

layers: domain (entities, value objects, domain errors â€” no framework or driver imports)
application (use cases / ports: e.g. TodoRepository; orchestration only)
infrastructure (SQLite adapter implementing ports; migration runner; file path resolution)
interface-adapters (Next.js App Router routes, server actions, DTO mapping to use case inputs)
clean_architecture.dependency_rule: Dependencies point inward: domain and application have no imports from `react`, `next/*`, SQLite drivers, or ORMs. Outer layers depend on inner abstractions (ports/interfaces defined in application); infrastructure implements those ports.

ddd.ubiquitous_language_notes: Todo: identifiable task with text (title/body per MVP) and completion state. Operations: create, list, update text, toggle complete, delete. Invalid input and persistence failures are explicit outcomes, not generic errors.
bounded_contexts: Todo management (single context for MVP; no cross-context coupling)
tactical: Aggregate root: Todo (enforce invariants: non-empty title where required, valid id for updates/deletes).
Value object optional: normalized title string where validation rules belong to the concept, not the framework.
Application services / use cases: one cohesive module per operation or thin facades calling domain + repository port.
No event sourcing or additional bounded contexts unless discovery changes.

singleton_policy: Do not hold a mutable global SQLite connection or module-level pool for the file DB. Prefer explicit factory (e.g. `createDbClient(path)`) or per-request acquisition from injected/configured path so tests can substitute an in-memory or temp file DB. Document any rare immutable singleton (e.g. logger) in delivery tasks if introduced.

frontend.atomic_design: Map UI to atoms (buttons, inputs, checkbox, text, spinner), molecules (todo row, inline edit, error banner), organisms (todo list shell, form). Pages/templates in App Router compose organisms only; lift list state and mutation handlers to page-level or dedicated hooks, not deep inside atoms.

constraints_for_implementation: Use styled-components with a typed `DefaultTheme` and centralized tokens (colors, spacing); add Next.js SSR/registry setup per the installed styled-components + App Router version so hydration is correct on Windows.
Place domain and application under dedicated folders (e.g. `src/domain`, `src/application`) with zero `react` / `next` / driver imports in domain.

SQLite file path and all related environment variables (e.g. `TODO_SQLITE_PATH`) are server-only: never use `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, never read in client components or in modules that ship to the browser; resolve and consume only from server-only surfaces (e.g. `src/server/*`, route handlers, server actions).

Versioned SQL migrations applied on startup or before first use; idempotent behavior on empty DB and on schema bumps; surface migration/open failures as user-safe messages without stack traces or internal paths in production UI.

Validate all mutating inputs at server boundaries (server actions or route handlers); return structured, safe messages for invalid input, empty title, and DB/migration failures (three explicit failure paths for UX proof).

Document Bun install, dev, build, lint/test, and Windows smoke steps in README; use Bun as the package/runtime manager for scripts. README must include dedicated sections for environment variables and the local SQLite database: `TODO_SQLITE_PATH`, file lifecycle (create/open), how to reset the DB safely, and caveats when the repo or `.data/` lives under OneDrive or other sync folders.

Infra-engineer is primary owner for this incrementâ€™s governance: confirm and enforce `.gitignore` coverage for `.data/` and `.env.local` (if not already); maintain `.env.example` including `TODO_SQLITE_PATH` with clear comments; provide a single documented command or infra-owned script that creates workspace-root `.env.local` with safe dev defaults. Never commit `.env.local`, secrets, or `.data/` contents.

Do not add a duplicate SQLite adapter, second persistence stack, or infra-side reimplementation of `TodoRepository` / driver wiring; infra work is limited to ignore rules, env templates, bootstrap of `.env.local`, documentation, and CI/docs workflow hooksâ€”not product persistence code.

Defer backend and application wiring changes unless a delivery task explicitly requires updating the SQLite path contract or composition (e.g. `src/server/todo-sqlite-path.ts` and dependent server bootstrap); otherwise keep persistence changes in existing backend/infrastructure tasks.

Split delivery tasks: domain vs application vs infrastructure vs UI vs migrations/quality-gate; each task lists `architecture_refs` from architecture-standards (e.g. clean-architecture, ddd-tactical, atomic-design, styled-components). Governance tasks should reference `clean-architecture` / `singleton-policy` where they touch env and DB file boundaries.

Delivery branch must satisfy acceptance checklist; `bun run lint` and `bun run test` pass; a fresh clone following README plus infra bootstrap steps can run locally without committing `.data/` or `.env.local`.
delivery_hints: Task A â€” Scaffold: Next App Router + TS + Bun (`package.json`, `tsconfig`, ESLint); README with install/dev/build and Windows smoke checklist for SQLite driver path.
Task B â€” Domain: Todo entity/aggregate, domain errors, invariants (e.g. empty title rejected); unit tests without DB.
Task C â€” Ports + use cases: `TodoRepository` interface and use cases (create, list, update text, toggle, delete); unit tests with in-memory fake repository.
Task D â€” Infrastructure: SQLite schema + migration files; adapter implementing `TodoRepository`; migration runner; integration tests against temp file DB.
Task E â€” Interface adapters: server actions or route handlers mapping requests to use cases, validation at boundary, map domain errors to user-facing messages.
Task F â€” UI: Atomic tree under `components/ui/{atoms,molecules,organisms}`; loading and error states visible; styled-components theme tokens file.
Task G â€” Quality gate: single documented command (or minimal set) for lint + tests; `architecture_refs` include `qa-validation` where appropriate.

Task H â€” Dev env governance (infra-engineer, primary): verify `.gitignore` for `.data/` and `.env.local`; extend README with Environment and Local SQLite sections (`TODO_SQLITE_PATH`, lifecycle, reset, OneDrive/sync caveats); keep `.env.example` aligned; implement documented creation of gitignored `.env.local` (one command and/or optional team-approved bootstrap script); ensure no adapter or driver code is added under infra-only paths.

Task I â€” Path contract (backend-agent, conditional only): if acceptance requires a changed path contract, edit `src/server/todo-sqlite-path.ts` and dependent server wiring only as specified by the taskâ€”no parallel SQLite stack.
Fresh-clone smoke: README steps + infra bootstrap produce a runnable dev setup; verify `bun run lint` and `bun run test` on the delivery branch.
